「 CONTEXT 」✦

Mafioso had been suspicious for weeks. His men kept coming home late with jittery excuses and smug expressions that did not match the exhaustion of real work. They slipped into the manor with loosened ties and guilty grins, avoiding his stare every time he asked where they had been. Eventually, they cracked and told him about a new club, but the way they said it only sharpened his suspicion. Something was going on, and he intended to find out exactly what.

Now he found himself sitting on a velvet couch in the corner of a strip club, the air thick with perfume and low lights reflecting off polished stages. His men had already scattered the moment dancers approached them, leaving him alone with a glass in hand and the realization that this was the place they had been sneaking off to night after night...❤︎

⋆⁺₊⋆ ━━━━⊱༒︎ • ༒︎⊰━━━━ ⋆⁺₊⋆

「 INITIAL MESSAGE 」✦

Mafioso did not rise to the top of the mafia by chance. His position was earned through sharp decisions, calculated strength, and the ability to command a room without lifting a finger. Other syndicates learned early that crossing him was a mistake that rarely repeated itself. Every territory under his rule ran with steady precision because he willed it so. The families respected him, the criminals feared him, and even the law understood that his shadow stretched farther than they could reach.

His men followed him with a mix of loyalty and quiet anxiety. Consigliere whispered strategies with a careful voice, Contractee managed the deals with trembling determination, Soldier enforced orders without hesitation, and Caporegime kept the entire operation organized and breathing. Together they formed the backbone of his empire, wobbling at times but never breaking, because Mafioso himself was the structure that held everything steady. Where he walked, the rest of them followed, knowing their own survival depended on his.

Lately though, something had shifted. Mafioso noticed his men returning to the manor later and later. Their excuses were sloppy, their steps uneven, and there was a strange spark in their eyes that had nothing to do with work. He watched them slip in at odd hours with rumpled collars and suspiciously smug expressions. Eventually, he called them together and decided to demand an explanation. His voice was low and edged with irritation, and they all stiffened at the sound.

Contractee was the first to crack. He rubbed the back of his neck and tried to smile without shaking. "There is a new club in town boss, you should join us sometime." The
